title: rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola
intro:
Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola, commonly known as Wild Rockrose, is a subspecies of the hybrid Rosa dumetorum, a plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a type of rose species known for its medicinal properties and is used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The health benefits of Wild Rockrose include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and soothe skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Wild Rockrose include flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects.

Note: There is limited information available about Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola. The provided information is based on general knowledge about the plant family Rosaceae and related species.
benefits: Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola has many benefits, such as promoting overall health by reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular well-being through its antioxidant properties, and supporting digestive health by regulating gut bacteria. Additionally, this plant has been known to have anti-cancer properties, reducing the risk of certain types of cancer, and its antimicrobial properties help prevent infections. The benefits of Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola also extend to mental well-being, with its adaptogenic properties helping to reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality, leading to a better overall quality of life. By incorporating this plant into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their physical and mental health, resulting in a more balanced and fulfilling life.
actions: Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including arthritis, skin irritations, and certain types of cancer. Its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain, allowing people to engage in physical activities and maintain their mobility, while its antioxidant properties can protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being, enabling individuals to lead healthier and more active lives. The antimicrobial properties of Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola can also prevent infections and promote wound healing, which can be particularly beneficial for people with compromised immune systems or those recovering from surgery. By harnessing the therapeutic actions of this plant, people can experience improved quality of life, enhanced physical function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
constituents: Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola has many active constituents, such as flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, phenolic acids like gallic acid and ellagic acid, and terpenoids like linalool and beta-sitosterol. These compounds are known to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, reduce the risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and cancer, and even prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola may also contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits for cardiovascular health, cognitive function, and even certain types of cancer. Overall, the medicinal constituents of Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola have the potential to improve people's daily lives by providing natural remedies for a range of health issues.
parts: Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which are traditionally used in herbal medicine. The leaves of Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers, also known as rose hips, are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as phenolic acids like gallic acid and ellagic acid, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots of the plant contain tannins, including gallic acid and epicatechin, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Rosa × dumetorum f. saxicola can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, or gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large quantities of its leaves or flowers can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to its volatile oils may cause dizziness, headaches, or respiratory issues. If not prepared correctly, its extracts may interact with medications, exacerbating conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes.
